items.mistake: Building paths with string concatenation like dir + '/' + name.
items.fix: Use Path(dir) / name so separators are correct on every OS.
items.why: Hard-coded slashes break on Windows and mishandle trailing separators.
items.example:
```python
# BAD: breaks on Windows; doubles slash if dir has trailing /
path = data_dir + '/' + filename

# GOOD: correct on every OS
path = Path(data_dir) / filename
```

items.mistake: Hard-coding secrets such as API keys in the source.
items.fix: Read them from os.environ and inject via the environment or a .env file.
items.why: Committed secrets leak and can't be rotated per-environment.
items.example:
```python
# BAD: ends up in git
openai.api_key = 'sk-abc123...'

# GOOD: per-env, never committed
openai.api_key = os.environ['OPENAI_API_KEY']
```

items.mistake: Calling mkdir() on a nested path that doesn't exist yet.
items.why: Without parents it raises FileNotFoundError; without exist_ok it raises if the dir exists.
items.example:
```python
Path('build/reports/q4').mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
# no error whether ancestors exist or not, or whether the dir already exists
```

items.fix: Open and iterate line-by-line (streaming) for large files.
items.why: read_text loads the entire file into memory at once.
items.example:
```python
# BAD: tries to load 5 GB into RAM
text = Path('huge.log').read_text()

# GOOD: constant memory
with Path('huge.log').open() as f:
    for line in f: process(line)
```
